decisions.basis: All 37 observations per scene had matching RGB/semantic/depth shapes for six cameras; intrinsics matched array dimensions, principal points were in bounds, v2c rotations were orthonormal with determinant approximately one, and calibration was constant over the rollout.
No non-finite or non-positive depth pixels were observed in either 37-frame six-camera rollout.
The two scenes show measurable but scene- and camera-dependent boundary co-variation. All modalities share the HUGSIM renderer, so this is not independent correctness evidence.

In scene-0041 the peak center-path risk was produced by a false car detection covering the ego hood / lower-image blur rather than the visible native truck. Scene-0138 also produced two low-confidence roadside false car detections.

scope_exceeds_evidence: no independent real RGB, labels, depth, calibration reference, or shared 3D correspondence was available. This rejects the stronger claim and is not a HUGSIM capability-failure finding.

claim_boundary: Accepted results establish bounded internal contracts and diagnostics only. They do not establish real-sensor accuracy, cross-camera 3D consistency, planning, control, or global HUGSIM credibility.
